data.text: Currently, your library which is a set of PDFs that we have uploaded for learning appears at the bottom. It should be appear in sidebar.

The user is asking about moving a "library" feature (which seems to be a set of PDFs for learning) from the bottom of the page to the sidebar. I need to understand the codebase structure first to see how this is currently implemented.

Let me start by exploring the codebase to understand the project structure and find where the library feature is defined.
I'll help you move the library from the bottom to the sidebar. Let me first explore the codebase to understand the current structure.

input.prompt:
Explore the codebase at /Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent to understand:
1. The project structure (what framework/technology is used)
2. Where the "library" feature (PDFs for learning) is currently implemented
3. Where the sidebar component is defined
4. How the layout is structured

Look for:
- Package.json or similar config files
- Component files related to "library", "sidebar", "layout"
- Any files mentioning PDFs or learning materials

Return a comprehensive summary of:
- The tech stack
- File paths for library components
- File paths for sidebar components  
- File paths for layout components
- How the library is currently rendered (at bottom vs sidebar)

Be very thorough - check src/, components/, pages/, app/ directories.

state.output:
I now have a complete picture of the entire codebase. Here is the comprehensive summary: --- ## Comprehensive Codebase Analysis: Qnify AI Learning Agent ### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE ``` /Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/ ├── backend/ # Fastify API server (Node.js + TypeScript) ├── frontend/ # React SPA (Vite + TypeScript) └── pdf-to-markdown/ # Python script for PDF conversion (Docling) ``` ### 2. TECH STACK **Frontend:** - **Framework:** React 19 with TypeScript 6 - **Build tool:** Vite 8 - **Routing:** react-router-dom 7 - **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 4 (via `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin) - **Component library:** shadcn/ui (base-vega style, Lucide icons) - **Other:** react-markdown, class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge, nanoid **Backend:** - **Framework:** Fastify 5 (Node.js + TypeScript) - **Database:** SQLite via better-sqlite3 - **AI/LLM:** LangChain with OpenAI (`@langchain/openai`, `@langchain/core`) - **Validation:** Zod 4 - **PDF upload:** @fastify/multipart **Utility:** - Python script (`pdf-to-markdown/convert-docling.py`) using Docling to convert PDFs to Markdown --- ### 3. LIBRARY FEATURE -- FILE PATHS The "library" feature is the core page for managing uploaded PDF learning documents. | Role | Absolute Path | |---|---| | **Library page (main view)** | `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/pages/library-page.tsx` | | **Document card component** | `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/components/document-card.tsx` | | **PDF upload dropzone** | `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/components/upload-dropzone.tsx` | | **Types (DocumentSummary, etc.)** | `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/lib/types.ts` | | **API client (listDocuments, uploadPdf, createPlan)** | `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/lib/api.ts` | | **Format helpers (difficultyLabel, formatDate)** | `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/lib/format.ts` | | **Backend documents service** | `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/backend/src/services/documents.ts` | | **Backend routes (document/plan endpoints)** | `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/backend/src/routes.ts` | | **Backend DB schema** | `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/backend/src/schema.sql` | --- ### 4. SIDEBAR COMPONENT -- FILE PATHS **There is NO sidebar component in this codebase.** A thorough search confirms: - No file is named "sidebar" anywhere in the project. - No component imports or renders a sidebar. - The CSS (`index.css`) defines sidebar CSS variables (e.g., `--sidebar`, `--sidebar-foreground`, `--sidebar-accent`, `--sidebar-border`, `--sidebar-ring`) but these come from the shadcn/ui theme defaults and are **never used by any component**. They are purely boilerplate from the shadcn theme configuration. - The only navigation element is a horizontal `
` bar inside the header of the `AppShell` component. --- ### 5. LAYOUT COMPONENT -- FILE PATHS | Role | Absolute Path | |---|---| | **App shell / layout wrapper** | `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/components/app-shell.tsx` | | **Router / entry point** | `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/main.tsx` | | **Global CSS (theme, sidebar vars)** | `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/index.css` | --- ### 6. HOW THE LAYOUT IS STRUCTURED The layout is a simple **header + main content** pattern with no sidebar: **`main.tsx`** -- Defines a `BrowserRouter` with `AppShell` as the root layout element and two child routes: - `/` --> `LibraryPage` (the default/index route) - `/learn/:planId` --> `LessonPage` **`app-shell.tsx`** -- The sole layout component: ```
(sticky top bar with logo + "Library" nav link) Qnify logo
Library <-- single nav item, horizontal
<-- child routes render here
``` Key layout characteristics: - **No sidebar at all.** Navigation is a single horizontal link in the sticky header. - The `
` area is centered with `max-w-7xl` and padding, using `` to render child routes. - The `LibraryPage` and `LessonPage` both render their content inside this single `
` column. --- ### 7. HOW THE LIBRARY IS CURRENTLY RENDERED The library is rendered as a **full-width main content area** (not a sidebar). Specifically, in `library-page.tsx`: 1. **Hero/Upload section** -- A decorative card at the top containing: - A "Human-in-the-loop learning agent" badge - Headline: "Turn any PDF into an interactive lesson." - An `` component for drag-and-drop PDF upload 2. **Document grid section** -- Below the hero, a grid of `` components: - Displays "Your library" heading with document count - 3-column responsive grid (`sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3`) - Each `` shows: filename, title, status (processing/ready/failed), creation date, markdown char count, attempt count, and action buttons (Start learning / Continue / Retake / Review plan) - Empty state shows a dashed border placeholder - Skeleton loading state shown during initial fetch - Polls every 3 seconds if any document has `status === "processing"` 3. **Flow:** Upload PDF --> auto-create plan --> navigate to `/learn/:planId` --> `LessonPage` renders the learning experience (plan review, quizzes, summary) --- ### 8. COMPLETE FILE INVENTORY **Frontend source files:** - `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/package.json` - `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/vite.config.ts` - `/Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/components.json` (shadcn config) - The entire application uses a flat single-column layout: a sticky header bar on top, and a centered `

data.text: All tasks are complete. Let me summarize what was done.

Done. I've moved the library to the sidebar. Here's what changed:

**New file:**
- `frontend/src/components/sidebar.tsx` — A sidebar component that displays the document list with status indicators, difficulty badges, and click-to-navigate functionality.

**Modified files:**
- `frontend/src/components/app-shell.tsx` — Restructured the layout from a single-column header+main to a sidebar+main layout using flexbox with `h-screen` and `overflow-hidden`.
- `frontend/src/pages/library-page.tsx` — Removed the document list section (now lives in the sidebar). Kept only the upload hero section.

**Also fixed (pre-existing):**
- `mcq-widget.tsx` and `summary-view.tsx` — Fixed invalid Badge variants (`"danger"` → `"destructive"`, `"success"` → `"default"`).

The layout now shows:
- **Left sidebar (272px):** Library document list with upload button
- **Right main area:** Upload hero and lesson content
